# clean-go: well-written Go utility with no rule violations
lang: go
file: clean_go_file.go
variant: stdlib
---
package util

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

var ErrNotFound = errors.New("not found")

// Config holds application configuration.
type Config struct {
	Dir    string
	Logger *Logger
}

// Logger provides structured info-level logging.
type Logger struct {
	prefix string
}

// NewLogger creates a new logger with the given prefix.
func NewLogger(prefix string) *Logger {
	return &Logger{prefix: prefix}
}

// Info writes a formatted info message to stdout.
func (l *Logger) Info(msg string) {
	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, "[%s] %s\n", l.prefix, msg)
}

// LoadConfig reads and returns a Config from the given path.
func LoadConfig(path string) (*Config, error) {
	if path == "" {
		return nil, ErrNotFound
	}
	return &Config{Dir: path, Logger: NewLogger("config")}, nil
}

// Add returns the sum of two integers.
func Add(a, b int) int {
	return a + b
}
